name: Minas de Riotinto, Aracena, Santa Ana La Real – circular route
name_original: Minas de Riotinto, Aracena, Santa Ana La Real – ruta circular
region_intro: Road cycling around Minas De Riotinto offers routes through a landscape shaped by 5,000 years of mining history, featuring distinct geological formations. The region is characterized by its "Martian" appearance, with the reddish Río Tinto river and vast, multi-colored open-pit mines like Corta Atalaya. Road cyclists can expect varied terrain across these unique mining landscapes and surrounding Mediterranean mountains. Best road cycling routes around Minas De Riotinto The most popular road cycling route is Minas de Riotinto, Aracena, Santa Ana La Real – circular route, a 54.1 miles (87.0 km) trail that takes 4 hours 30 minutes to complete. This route navigates through diverse terrain, showcasing the region's unique mining heritage and natural features. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Nerva, El Madroño,, a difficult 36.3 miles (58.5 km) path. This route provides views of the distinctive Río Tinto river and the surrounding transformed landscapes. Local road cyclists also love the Nerva, Minas de Riotinto, Zalamea La Real – circular route, a 33.2 miles (53.5 km) trail leading through mining landscapes and the Río Tinto basin, often completed in about 3 hours 4 minutes. Road cycling around Minas De Riotinto is defined by its "Martian" landscapes, the distinct Río Tinto river, and vast open-pit mines. The network offers options for moderate to difficult ability levels, with 3 moderate and 12 difficult routes available. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 140 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Minas De Riotinto's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Minas De Riotinto?
A: Road cycling in Minas De Riotinto offers varied terrain, primarily characterized by its unique "Martian" landscapes. You'll encounter routes through areas shaped by 5,000 years of mining history, featuring distinct geological formations, the reddish Río Tinto river, and vast, multi-colored open-pit mines. The broader region also includes Mediterranean mountains, offering diverse natural environments.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists in Minas De Riotinto?
A: Yes, Minas De Riotinto is well-suited for experienced cyclists seeking a challenge. Out of the 15 available road cycling routes, 12 are classified as difficult. These routes often feature significant elevation gains and varied terrain. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991710772/minas-de-riotinto-aracena-santa-ana-la-real-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Minas de Riotinto, Aracena, Santa Ana La Real – circular route&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 54.1 miles (87.0 km) with over 1,500 meters of elevation gain.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly road cycling routes in Minas De Riotinto?
A: While the region is known for its challenging terrain, there are a few moderate options available. Out of the 15 road cycling routes, 3 are classified as moderate. These routes offer a less strenuous experience while still allowing you to explore the unique landscapes. For a truly easy experience, the Vía Verde de Riotinto, though primarily for mountain biking, runs parallel to the Río Tinto and offers a flatter, scenic path.

Q: What natural features can I see while road cycling in Minas De Riotinto?
A: The region is famous for its extraordinary natural features. You can witness the unique reddish hue of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4402997" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Río Tinto&lt;/a&gt;, whose waters are known for their high acidity. The landscape is also dominated by gigantic, multi-colored open-pit mines like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5287308" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Corta Atalaya Mine&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5300427" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña del Hierro&lt;/a&gt;, which offer dramatic views. The area's distinct geology has even attracted NASA for research into other planets.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or viewpoints along the cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region is rich in historical mining landmarks and offers several viewpoints. You can see the vast &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5287308" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Corta Atalaya Mine&lt;/a&gt;, once considered the largest opencast mine globally, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5300427" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña del Hierro&lt;/a&gt;, which offers an underground walk. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5082202" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Touristic Railway for Rio Tinto Mines&lt;/a&gt; and the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5345695" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cachán Bridge&lt;/a&gt; are also notable points of interest, showcasing the area's mining heritage.

Q: What is the best time of year to go road cycling in Minas De Riotinto?
A: While specific seasonal conditions are not detailed, the Mediterranean climate suggests spring and autumn are ideal for road cycling in Minas De Riotinto. These seasons typically offer milder temperatures, making longer rides more comfortable. Summers can be very hot, and winters might bring cooler, potentially wetter conditions, though the region generally experiences many sunny days.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Minas De Riotinto?
A: Yes, several circular road cycling routes are available, allowing you to start and end your ride in the same location. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991710772/minas-de-riotinto-aracena-santa-ana-la-real-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Minas de Riotinto, Aracena, Santa Ana La Real – circular route&lt;/a&gt; (87.0 km) and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991344075/nerva-minas-de-riotinto-zalamea-la-real-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nerva, Minas de Riotinto, Zalamea La Real – circular route&lt;/a&gt; (53.5 km). These loops are excellent for exploring the region's unique landscapes and mining heritage.

Q: Are there long-distance road cycling routes available in Minas De Riotinto?
A: Yes, for those looking for longer rides, Minas De Riotinto offers several extended road cycling routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31857282" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Zarandas-Naya Station – Riotinto Mines loop from Zarandas-Naya&lt;/a&gt; is a significant route covering approximately 97.2 km. Another substantial option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991710772/minas-de-riotinto-aracena-santa-ana-la-real-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Minas de Riotinto, Aracena, Santa Ana La Real – circular route&lt;/a&gt;, which is 87.0 km long.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Minas De Riotinto?
A: Road cyclists using komoot frequently praise the unique and dramatic landscapes of Minas De Riotinto. The "Martian" appearance, the distinct reddish Río Tinto river, and the vast open-pit mines create an unparalleled backdrop for cycling. Many appreciate the challenging nature of the routes and the opportunity to explore a region rich in history and geological wonders.

Q: Can I find places to eat or drink near the road cycling routes?
A: While specific cafes or pubs are not detailed in the route information, the towns and villages within and around Minas De Riotinto, such as Nerva, Minas de Riotinto, and Zalamea La Real, are likely to offer local establishments where cyclists can refuel and relax. It's advisable to check local listings or plan your route to pass through these settlements.

Q: Is public transport available to access the road cycling routes in Minas De Riotinto?
A: Information on specific public transport links directly to route starting points is not provided. However, the main towns in the region, such as Minas de Riotinto and Nerva, typically have bus connections to larger cities. Cyclists planning to use public transport should research local bus schedules to determine the best way to reach their desired starting points.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints along the road cycling routes that offer panoramic views?
A: Yes, the mining landscapes themselves offer dramatic panoramic views. The vast open-pit mines like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5287308" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Corta Atalaya Mine&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5300427" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña del Hierro&lt;/a&gt; provide stunning vistas of the multi-colored excavations. Additionally, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20122208" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Berrocal Viewpoint – El Madroño loop from Nerva&lt;/a&gt; suggest dedicated viewpoints along the way, allowing cyclists to appreciate the unique geology and scenery.
tips: The traditional popular architecture of the area stands out. You must see the parish church of Santa Ana, in a Renaissance style and with a dome and a tower worth visiting, and the town's lime kiln, an old lime kiln that refers to both the culture and history of the town.

This beautiful town is located between Alájar and Aracena, in the center of the Sierra de Aracena y Picos de Aroche Natural Park, one of the most important protected areas in the Community. Its cobbled streets, the Parish Church of San Juan Bautista, the public laundry and its crowded houses stand out.

The town of Aracena is dominated by its castle, where it is located on a hill and where you can see the group of houses and surroundings that surround it. The Castle of Aracena is accompanied by the Iglesia del Castillo.

waypoints.name: Santa Ana la Real Town Center
